input.description: Extract text from March 14 postmortem HTML

content.content: Postmortem: 2026-03-14 search outage (t-acme) Postmortem: 2026-03-14 search outage (t-acme) On 2026-03-14 between 09:00 and 11:00 UTC, tenant t-acme experienced elevated 503s. Incident tracker: https://status.meridianlabs.example/incidents/INC-2417 . A misbehaving client at 203.0.113.42 exhausted the upstream pool. All failing requests share trace id 7f3d9a2e-5b1c-4e8f-9a6d-2c4b8e1f7a30. Impacted user u-1042 reported export failures. Worker memory limit was raised from 512 MB to 1024 MB. Contact dana.rivera@meridianlabs.example or sylvia.chen@northgate.example for the full timeline.

tool_use_result.stdout: Postmortem: 2026-03-14 search outage (t-acme) Postmortem: 2026-03-14 search outage (t-acme) On 2026-03-14 between 09:00 and 11:00 UTC, tenant t-acme experienced elevated 503s. Incident tracker: https://status.meridianlabs.example/incidents/INC-2417 . A misbehaving client at 203.0.113.42 exhausted the upstream pool. All failing requests share trace id 7f3d9a2e-5b1c-4e8f-9a6d-2c4b8e1f7a30. Impacted user u-1042 reported export failures. Worker memory limit was raised from 512 MB to 1024 MB. Contact dana.rivera@meridianlabs.example or sylvia.chen@northgate.example for the full timeline.

content.text: The postmortem gives the core story. Now let me corroborate it across the other sources — logs, events, notes, and the database.

cd /tmp/xerj-discover/corpus
echo "== nginx logs with the IP =="; grep -rc '203.0.113.42' logs/nginx/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v ':0'

echo "== app logs with trace id =="; grep -rc '7f3d9a2e-5b1c-4e8f-9a6d-2c4b8e1f7a30' logs/app/ | grep -v ':0'
echo "== sample app lines =="; grep -rh '7f3d9a2e-5b1c-4e8f-9a6d-2c4b8e1f7a30' logs/app/ | head -5

content.text:
All sources line up. Here's the reconstruction.

## What happened on the morning of 2026-03-14

**A misbehaving client exhausted the upstream connection pool, causing a search outage with elevated 503 errors for tenant `t-acme` between 09:00 and 11:00 UTC.**

### The story across sources

- **Postmortem** (`docs/html/postmortems/2026-03-14-search-outage.html`) is the anchor: on 2026-03-14, 09:00–11:00 UTC, tenant **t-acme** saw elevated 503s after a misbehaving client at **203.0.113.42** exhausted the upstream pool. All failing requests carried trace ID **7f3d9a2e-5b1c-4e8f-9a6d-2c4b8e1f7a30**; user **u-1042** reported export failures. As remediation, the worker memory limit was raised from 512 MB to 1024 MB.
- **Nginx access log** (`logs/nginx/access-2026-03-14.log`) confirms the offender: 203.0.113.42 hit `t-acme` search and health endpoints and got **503s at 09:30:09, 10:11:03, and 10:49:17 UTC** — squarely inside the stated window — with the incident URL appearing in the referrer field.
- **App log** (`logs/app/app-2026-03-14.log`) has ERROR lines at **09:43:25** (ingest-svc) and **10:29:32** (billing-svc) for `tenant=t-acme` carrying the same trace ID, with the message "upstream pool exhausted from 203.0.113.42; see …/INC-2417".
- **Event stream** (`events/2026/03/14/events-10.jsonl`) contains a burst of **25 error events for t-acme between 10:00:03 and 10:01:15 UTC**, all from client IP 203.0.113.42 and all tagged with the same trace ID.
- **Audit trail** (`db/backup-2026-04-01.sql`, `audit_log` row 1) ties the same IP and trace ID to earlier activity by user u-1447 on 2026-03-09 — the client had been active before the outage.
- **On-call note** (`notes/oncall/note-0120.txt`) links the incident URL and records the pre-incident budget "workers get 512 MB each," matching the postmortem's remediation. **KB article** `docs/html/kb/kb-0010.html` and `config/contacts.yaml` also reference the status page; contacts.yaml identifies the escalation contacts (dana.rivera@meridianlabs.example, sylvia.chen@northgate.example) named in the postmortem.

### Key identifiers

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Affected tenant | `t-acme` |
| Time window | 2026-03-14, 09:00–11:00 UTC (observed activity 09:30–10:49) |
| Offending client IP | 203.0.113.42 |
| Trace / request ID | 7f3d9a2e-5b1c-4e8f-9a6d-2c4b8e1f7a30 |
| Incident URL | https://status.meridianlabs.example/incidents/INC-2417 |
| Reporting user | u-1042 (export failures) |
| Remediation | Worker memory limit raised 512 MB → 1024 MB |

One caveat for accuracy: baseline 503s and app errors occur at a fairly constant rate all day in these logs, so the "elevated" signal is best evidenced by the incident-tagged records themselves (the trace-ID'd error burst at 10:00–10:01 and the IP's 503s), which all fall inside the postmortem's stated window.
